item.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth to Quicken in Second Half of Next Year
item.description: Euro-area pay growth is set to accelerate in the second half, adding to the challenges the European Central Bank faces as war rages in the Middle East.

item.title: Hedge Funds Feed Dollar Gains as US, Iran Exchange Threats
item.description: The dollar advanced and traders bet on further gains for the reserve currency as the war in the Middle East entered its fourth week and the US exchanged threats with Iran.

item.title: Strategists Unfazed by War Expect European Stocks to Bounce
item.description: Market strategists are sanguine about mounting inflation fears caused by the Iran war and expect European stocks to return to record highs, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Laopu Gold Sales, Profit Beat as Gold Rally Fuels Demand
item.description: Laopu Gold Co.’s full-year revenue and profit surged as its jewelry pieces and gold ornaments gained huge popularity from the country’s middle class shoppers, aided by soaring gold prices.

item.title: Dangote Ships Fuel to Five Nations After Reaching Full Capacity
item.description: Nigeria’s Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals has begun exporting fuel across Africa after reaching full production, shipping about a dozen cargoes to markets as far as Tanzania.

item.title: China Copper Inventories Plunge as Falling Prices Aid Demand
item.description: Chinese copper inventories had their biggest weekly drop this year, according to Mysteel Global, as a rapid slump in prices of the metal due to the Iran war buoyed demand.

item.title: European Stocks Sink Into Correction on Trump’s Iran Escalation
item.description: European stocks slumped, with the Stoxx Europe 600 Index on course for a correction from its February record high, as the conflict in the Middle East escalated.

item.title: Bonds Lose $2.5 Trillion in Iran War Wipeout That Mirrors 2022
item.description: The specter of stagflation caused by the Iran war has wiped out more than $2.5 trillion from the value of global bonds in March, on track for the biggest monthly loss in more than three years.

item.title: Traders Fully Price Four Quarter-Point Hikes From BOE in 2026
item.description: Traders are fully pricing a full percentage point of Bank of England monetary tightening in 2026 for the first time, as the Iran conflict continues to fuel inflationary pressures.

item.title: Danone to Buy UK Protein Drinks Maker Huel in Nutrition Push
item.description: Danone SA will acquire UK-based fortified drinks maker Huel, as part of the French food company’s push into functional nutrition.

item.title: What the Iran War Means for Dubai’s Luxury Boom
item.description: The future of Gulf capital.

item.title: Balderton-Backed Startup Dash0 Bags $110 Million to Expand in US
item.description: Dash0 has raised $110 million at a $1 billion valuation in a Balderton Capital-led round to expand its monitoring platform, used to detect problems in software systems, across the US.

item.title: UBS Could Slow Investments on Iran War Uncertainty, Ermotti Says
item.description: UBS Group AG Chief Executive Officer Sergio Ermotti said the Iran war could force him to pare back spending, although it won’t fundamentally alter the bank’s overall strategy.

item.title: Bain Capital’s Bridge Data Centres Seeks Up to $6 Billion Loan
item.description: Bain Capital-owned Bridge Data Centres is in talks with lenders for a potential loan of up to $6 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be one of the largest-ever borrowings in Asia for data centers.

item.title: Japan’s Labor Union Wins Wage Hike Topping 5% for Third Year
item.description: Japan’s largest labor union group said its workers secured an average pay increase exceeding 5% for a third straight year, a development that likely will keep the central bank on track for another interest rate hike in coming months.

item.title: European Gas Resumes Gains as US and Iran Trade Hormuz Threats
item.description: European natural gas futures resumed gains as traders watched the US and Iran exchange threats over the Strait of Hormuz, adding nervousnesses to an already volatile market.

item.title: Gilts Face Worst Month Since Liz Truss as Oil Spike Jolts UK Market
item.description: UK government bonds are headed for their worst month since the historic rout that led to the ouster of former Prime Minister Liz Truss, as the war in the Middle East drives up energy costs and fuels bets on interest-rate hikes.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway to Stake $1.8 Billion in Insurer Tokio Marine
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. will invest 287.4b yen ($1.8 billion) in insurer Tokio Marine Holdings Inc., marking a significant increase in the US conglomerate’s exposure to the Japanese market.

item.title: Grab Strikes $600 Million Deal for Foodpanda Taiwan Business
item.description: Grab Holdings Ltd. agreed to buy Delivery Hero SE’s Foodpanda operations in Taiwan for $600 million, a deal that marks its first foray outside of its Southeast Asian home market.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Middle East Tensions Escalate
India Swaps Turn More Aggressive on Rate Hikes, Boosting Yields
item.description: India’s swap markets are signaling a more aggressive shift toward interest-rate increases as oil prices surge, pointing to prospects for further gains in bond yields.

item.title: Australian Regulators Stress Vigilance as Global Risks Intensify
item.description: Australia’s top financial regulators warned on Monday that an escalation in global geopolitical tensions could amplify financial stability risks, emphasizing the need for continued vigilance.

item.title: Two Indian LPG Carriers Are in Transit Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Two Indian-flagged vessels carrying liquefied petroleum gas are making their way through the Strait of Hormuz, taking a route that hews closely to the Iranian coastline, ship-tracking data show.

item.title: Fortescue Bets on China Ties as CMRG Broadens Iron Ore Influence
item.description: Miner Fortescue Ltd. expects its extensive use of Chinese capital and mining equipment to set it apart from rival iron ore producers as Beijing seeks to extend its influence in the market, Chief Executive Officer Dino Otranto said in an interview.

item.title: Singapore’s Family-Run Firms Lack Pay Transparency, Study Shows
item.description: Singapore-listed companies are replete with executive directors that are either substantial shareholders or related to them, and firms aren’t disclosing enough about how pay is decided.

item.title: ECB Alert to Second-Round Effects From Iran War, Guindos Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is on alert for the fallout on prices from the Iran war, Vice President Luis de Guindos told El Mundo newspaper.

item.title: Corn Hits Two-Week High as Iran War Threatens Fertilizer Supply
item.description: Corn futures rose to a two-week high as disrupted fertilizer supplies from the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz casts doubts over the size of the upcoming US crop.

item.title: Private Capital Turns to Heavy Assets as Software Trade Dims
item.description: Private capital firms are starting to swap software systems for hard hats as the artificial intelligence boom forces the industry into a quick rethink of its priorities.

item.title: Veteran Crisis Caller Shin May Give Bank of Korea a Hawkish Tone
item.description: Hyun Song Shin, the longtime Bank for International Settlements heavyweight now nominated to take the helm of the Bank of Korea, is known for a keen focus on financial market stability and his ability to spot trouble early.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Push Back Calls for China Rate Cuts During War
item.description: Some of the biggest US banks raised their projections for China’s inflation this year and pushed back predictions for its next interest-rate cut, as the escalating conflict in Iran sends oil prices higher.
